lessons.title: What is Accessibility?
lessons.content:
Web Accessibility Overview

Web accessibility (A11y) means that websites, tools, and technologies are designed and developed so that people with disabilities can use them.

Accessibility is not just for people with permanent disabilities. It also benefits people with temporary or situational limitations.

lessons.title: Accessible Images
lessons.content:
The alt Attribute

The alt attribute provides a text alternative for an image if it cannot be displayed.

Screen readers read the alt text to users who cannot see the image.

<img src="dog.jpg" alt="A golden retriever puppy playing in the grass">

lessons.title: Semantic HTML
lessons.content:
Use Semantic Elements

Semantic elements (like <nav>, <header>, <main>, <footer>) help screen readers understand the structure and purpose of different parts of a web page.

quiz.question: Which HTML attribute provides alternative text for images?
What is the primary purpose of ARIA (Accessible Rich Internet Applications)?
options: To provide more information to screen readers
To add animations to the UI

quiz.question: Which of the following is true regarding color contrast for accessibility?
options: High contrast is essential for users with visual impairments
Low contrast is better for night mode
Color should be the only way to convey information

quiz.question: Which HTML tag is most appropriate for a button that triggers an action?
